_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 1-6 Anaphylaxis/Allergic Reaction (Revised 08/11/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan General Treatment Protocol Section 1-6: Anaphylaxis/Allergic Reaction — MDHHS Approved 8/11/23, Reviewed 2023

sections.title: A. Initial Assessment
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 1-6: Anaphylaxis/Allergic Reaction — Section A
points: Follow General Pre-Hospital Care-Treatment Protocol
Pediatric patients (< 14 years of age): utilize MI MEDIC cards for appropriate medication dosage; when unavailable, use pediatric dosing listed within protocol
Ensure ALS response
Determine if anaphylaxis/severe allergic reaction (wheezing AND/OR hypotension) or allergic reaction (itching, hives) — management differs
Determine substance or source of exposure; remove patient from source if known and able

sections.title: B. Anaphylaxis / Severe Allergic Reaction
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 1-6: Anaphylaxis/Allergic Reaction — Section B
points: Assist patient in use of their own prescribed epinephrine auto-injector, if available
Administer epinephrine auto-injector IM (MCA approval required — see MCA Selections below)

PEDIATRIC DOSING — auto-injector IM: Contact Medical Control if child appears to weigh < 10 kg (approx. 20 lbs.) prior to epi administration if possible; administer pediatric auto-injector (0.15 mg) IM if child weighs 10–30 kg (approx. 20–60 lbs.); administer standard auto-injector (0.3 mg) IM for children > 30 kg or adults
May repeat epinephrine auto-injector IM ONE TIME after 3–5 minutes if patient remains hypotensive and auto-injector is available

DRAW-UP EPINEPHRINE IM (MCA approval required — must complete MCA-approved training): Contact Medical Control if child weighs < 10 kg; administer 0.15 mg (0.15 mL) IM of epinephrine 1 mg/mL if child weighs 10–30 kg; administer 0.3 mg (0.3 mL) IM of epinephrine 1 mg/mL for adults and children > 30 kg; may repeat once after 3–5 minutes if patient remains hypotensive; maximum of 2 total epinephrine doses (prescribed auto-injector + EMS auto-injector + draw-up combined)
NOTE: BLS not carrying epinephrine auto-injector MUST participate in draw-up epinephrine
If wheezing and/or airway constriction: administer albuterol 2.5 mg / 3 mL NS nebulized (per MCA selection — may be EMT skill) per Medication Administration-Medication Protocol

If wheezing continues: administer nebulized albuterol 2.5 mg / 3 mL NS AND ipratropium 500 mcg / 2.5 mL NS per Medication Administration-Medication Protocol (per MCA selection — may be Specialist skill)

For patients with hypotension: administer NS or LR IV/IO fluid bolus (Specialist skill) — refer to Vascular Access and IV Fluid Therapy-Procedure Protocol and Shock-Treatment Protocol; Adults: up to 1 liter wide open; Pediatrics: 20 mL/kg based on signs/symptoms of shock; slow to KVO when SBP > 90 mmHg

Hypotensive patients should receive additional IV/IO fluid boluses as indicated: Adults repeat to maximum 2 liters; Pediatrics repeat 20 mL/kg to maximum 40 mL/kg; monitor for pulmonary edema — if presents, stop fluids and contact Medical Control

If hypotension persists/unresponsive to fluid bolus OR severe respiratory distress unresponsive to nebulized treatment: administer push-dose epinephrine IV/IO (Specialist skill only); Adults: 20 mcg (2 mL of epi 10 mcg/mL) IV/IO, repeat q3–5 min, titrate SBP > 90 mmHg; Pediatrics: 1 mcg/kg (0.1 mL of epi 10 mcg/mL) IV/IO, max 10 mcg (1 mL), repeat q3–5 min

sections.title: C. Allergic Reaction (Not Anaphylaxis) OR After Epinephrine Administration
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 1-6: Anaphylaxis/Allergic Reaction — Section C
points: Administer diphenhydramine (Specialist skill): Adults 50 mg IM or IV/IO; Pediatrics 1 mg/kg IM/IV/IO (maximum dose 50 mg)
If wheezing and albuterol not already administered: administer albuterol 2.5 mg / 3 mL NS nebulized (per MCA selection — may be EMT skill)
If wheezing continues: administer nebulized albuterol 2.5 mg / 3 mL NS AND ipratropium 500 mcg / 2.5 mL NS (per MCA selection — may be Specialist skill)

Administer prednisone tablet 50 mg PO to adults and children > 6 years of age (per MCA selection — additional medication option); if prednisone not available, patient is < 6 y/o, or patient unable to receive PO: administer methylprednisolone IV/IO/IM (Specialist skill): Adults 125 mg; Pediatrics 2 mg/kg (max 125 mg)

sections.title: D. Refractory Patients
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 1-6: Anaphylaxis/Allergic Reaction — Section D
points: Patients unresponsive to treatment: contact Medical Control

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must designate which level may administer epinephrine auto-injector; MCA must maintain roster submitted to MDHHS
MCA must designate which levels may administer draw-up epinephrine; personnel must complete MCA-approved training; MCA must maintain roster submitted to MDHHS
MCA must indicate whether EMT may administer nebulized albuterol
MCA must indicate whether Specialist (AEMT) may administer albuterol/ipratropium combination
MCA may approve prednisone PO as an additional medication option for adults and children > 6 y/o

key_drugs.name: Epinephrine Auto-Injector
key_drugs.dose: 0.15 mg (peds 10–30 kg) or 0.3 mg (> 30 kg / adult); contact Medical Control if < 10 kg
key_drugs.contraindications: No absolute contraindications in life-threatening anaphylaxis
key_drugs.scope: MCA selection required — MFR option available; assisting with patient's own auto-injector is EMT scope
key_drugs.notes: Hold device in place 10 seconds after activation. May repeat once after 3–5 minutes if patient remains hypotensive. Maximum 2 total epi doses (all sources combined).

key_drugs.name: Epinephrine IM (Draw-Up)
key_drugs.dose: 0.15 mg (0.15 mL) of 1 mg/mL for 10–30 kg; 0.3 mg (0.3 mL) of 1 mg/mL for > 30 kg or adult
key_drugs.contraindications: No absolute contraindications in life-threatening anaphylaxis; contact Medical Control if < 10 kg
key_drugs.scope: MCA selection required — MFR and BLS options available; training required
key_drugs.notes: May repeat once after 3–5 minutes if patient remains hypotensive. Maximum 2 total IM doses including any auto-injector doses.

key_drugs.name: Albuterol
key_drugs.dose: 2.5 mg / 3 mL NS nebulized — same dose for adult and pediatric
key_drugs.indication: Wheezing and/or airway constriction in anaphylaxis or allergic reaction
key_drugs.scope: Per MCA selection — EMT option available

key_drugs.name: Ipratropium (combined with Albuterol)
key_drugs.dose: 500 mcg / 2.5 mL NS nebulized combined with albuterol 2.5 mg / 3 mL NS
key_drugs.scope: Per MCA selection — Specialist option available
key_drugs.notes: Use only when initial albuterol alone is insufficient.

key_drugs.name: Diphenhydramine
key_drugs.dose: Adults: 50 mg IM or IV/IO; Pediatrics: 1 mg/kg IM/IV/IO (max 50 mg)
key_drugs.indication: Allergic reaction symptoms; adjunct after epinephrine in anaphylaxis
key_drugs.notes: Administer after epinephrine in anaphylaxis, or as first-line in mild allergic reaction without anaphylaxis criteria.

key_drugs.name: Methylprednisolone
key_drugs.dose: Adults: 125 mg IV/IO/IM; Pediatrics: 2 mg/kg IV/IO/IM (max 125 mg)
key_drugs.indication: Anaphylaxis/allergic reaction when prednisone not available or not appropriate
key_drugs.notes: Use when prednisone not available, patient < 6 y/o, or patient unable to receive PO medication.

deterioration_flags: Hypotension not responding to epinephrine — ensure ALS; repeat epinephrine if second dose not yet given; initiate IV/IO fluids (Specialist); consider push-dose epi IV/IO (Specialist)
Respiratory distress not improving after albuterol — contact ALS; push-dose epi or albuterol/ipratropium (ALS/Paramedic skills)
Airway compromise (angioedema, stridor) — prepare for airway management; BVM ventilation if needed; contact ALS immediately
Deteriorating LOC — maintain airway; position supine with legs elevated if no respiratory compromise; ALS priority response
Return of anaphylaxis symptoms after initial response — consider repeat epinephrine if second dose not yet given
scope_notes: Assist with patient's own epi auto-injector: EMT scope (all levels)
Epinephrine auto-injector IM (EMS-administered): MCA selection required — MFR option
Draw-up epinephrine IM: MCA selection required — MFR and BLS options; training required
Albuterol nebulized: MCA selection required — EMT option
Albuterol/ipratropium nebulized: MCA selection required — Specialist option
IV/IO access and fluid bolus: Specialist skill
Prednisone PO: MCA additional medication option (requires MCA selection)
